channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19948] fallocate from lustre client can use up reserved space on ldiskfs based OST
item.description:
A lustre client can use fallocate to use up all the reserved blocks on ldiskfs based OST.

When such OST is remounted the mount operations fails with ENOSPC error.

Since the mount fails there is no way to free up space on the OST.

Here are the steps to repro the issue:

  1. From lustre client create multiple files on an OST (using striping policy that targets the OST).
  2. Use fallocate to allocate blocks to the file without increasing logical file size, until the OST is completely filled. E.g. fallocate -n -l <new size> <file path>
  1. Unmount the OST and try to mount it again. The mount operation fails with ENOSPC error.

Here is the output of tune2fs for the OST that can be filled up using fallocate:

 tune2fs -l /dev/mapper/mpatha
tune2fs 1.47.1-wc1 (28-May-2024)
Filesystem volume name:   lustrefs-OST0000
Last mounted on:          /
Filesystem UUID:          4a97c9ba-851e-4bd2-8143-95eb9dc06a16
Filesystem magic number:  0xEF53
Filesystem revision #:    1 (dynamic)
Filesystem features:      has_journal ext_attr resize_inode dir_index filetype extent mmp flex_bg large_dir sparse_super large_file huge_file uninit_bg dir_nlink quota project
Filesystem flags:         signed_directory_hash
Default mount options:    user_xattr acl
Filesystem state:         clean
Errors behavior:          Continue
Filesystem OS type:       Linux
Inode count:              7692800
Block count:              13107200
Reserved block count:     655360
Overhead clusters:        1237856
Free blocks:              4096
Free inodes:              7691795
First block:              0
Block size:               4096
Fragment size:            4096
Reserved GDT blocks:      1019
Blocks per group:         32768
Fragments per group:      32768
Inodes per group:         19232
Inode blocks per group:   2404
Flex block group size:    256
Filesystem created:       Thu Nov 20 19:52:07 2025
Last mount time:          Tue Feb 24 20:00:38 2026
Last write time:          Tue Feb 24 20:02:18 2026
Mount count:              13
Maximum mount count:      -1
Last checked:             Thu Nov 20 19:52:07 2025
Check interval:           0 (<none>)
Lifetime writes:          76 GB
Reserved blocks uid:      0 (user root)
Reserved blocks gid:      0 (group root)
First inode:              11
Inode size:               512
Required extra isize:     32
Desired extra isize:      32
Journal inode:            8
Default directory hash:   half_md4
Directory Hash Seed:      1a41458f-8e37-4d1e-9a60-7dd0256219be
Journal backup:           inode blocks
MMP block number:         30390
MMP update interval:      1
User quota inode:         3
Group quota inode:        4
Project quota inode:      12

mount -t lustre /dev/mapper/mpatha /mnt/ost0
mount.lustre: mount /dev/mapper/mpatha at /mnt/ost0 failed: Input/output error
Is the MGS running?

Here are the relevant logs of failure during mount:

00080000:00020000:26.0:1772566352.959373:0:2711864:0:(osd_io.c:2154:osd_ldiskfs_write_record()) lustrefs-OST0000/: error reading offset 0 (block 0, size 8192, offs 0), credits 36/36: rc = -28
00000040:00000001:26.0:1772566352.959381:0:2711864:0:(llog_osd.c:656:llog_osd_write_rec()) Process leaving via out_unlock (rc=18446744073709551588 : -28 : 0xffffffffffffffe4)
00000040:00000001:26.0:1772566352.959382:0:2711864:0:(llog_osd.c:702:llog_osd_write_rec()) Process leaving via out (rc=18446744073709551588 : -28 : 0xffffffffffffffe4)
00000040:00000001:26.0:1772566352.959383:0:2711864:0:(llog_osd.c:772:llog_osd_write_rec()) Process leaving (rc=18446744073709551588 : -28 : ffffffffffffffe4)
00000040:00000001:26.0:1772566352.959384:0:2711864:0:(llog.c:1174:llog_write_rec()) Process leaving (rc=18446744073709551588 : -28 : ffffffffffffffe4)
 
The ask here is to have enough blocks reserved on OST so the mount operation can succeed. Lustre clients should not be able to use up the reserved blocks which prevents OST mount failures.
item.summary: fallocate from lustre client can use up reserved space on ldiskfs based OST

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment: Looks like the mount failure due to ENOSPC has been addressed by LU-14714
